mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. This reflects a healthy consumption-driven economy.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative more than 55% of the time when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% and remains below for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% and stays there for at least 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL is below 215 and FEDFUNDS is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of strong economic growth, indicated by robust GDP expansion, positive housing starts data signals increasing consumer and business confidence. This translates quickly into positive short-term equity market sentiment over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized and HOUST month-over-month growth is positive
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.10) during periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and HOUST shows month-over-month increases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing activity and business investment, signaling strong economic momentum that supports higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer base. This underpins corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a typical monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or near zero (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening. This increases borrowing costs and dampens economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25pp in 3 months and CPIAUCSL is above 215
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises significantly while CPIAUCSL is above its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer confidence and spending, which can quickly translate into negative market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 declines as investors anticipate lower corporate earnings over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a month-over-month decline in RSAFS exceeding 0.5%.
